channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Sean Manaea to re-sign with Mets for $75 million over 3 years, AP source says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean Manaea is set to return to the New York Mets on a $75 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the agreement told The Associated Press. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity Monday because the deal was subject to a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netflix is airing 2 NFL games on Christmas Day. Here's what to know
item.description: Netflix will have one of its biggest days since the site launched in 1998 when it carries two NFL games for the first time on Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Houston Texans wide receiver Tank Dell released from hospital after severe knee injury
item.description: It’s the second straight year Tank Dell has sustained what is almost certain to be a season-ending injury.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: San Jose State preps for another bowl game in paradise — without some key players
item.description: The Spartans will face South Florida without superstar receiver Nick Nash and top cornerback DJ Harvey.
item.dc:creator: Christian Vieyra, The Mercury News

item.title: Mickey, Minnie, Goofy and Wemby: Spurs-Knicks Christmas game is also an animated one at Disney World
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a Christmas Day basketball game at Walt Disney World, featuring Mickey, Minnie, Goofy and Wemby. An animated game, anyway.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On Football: Falcons and Buccaneers flip spots atop the NFC South and playoff races tighten up
item.description: The 49ers, Cowboys and Cardinals are out of the playoff race. The Falcons took control of their hopes with help, and the Colts, Dolphins and Bengals kept slim chances alive. The NFL’s playoff picture became clearer Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dallas Cowboys make owner Jerry Jones proud after being eliminated from the playoff chase
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — The Dallas Cowboys were officially eliminated from the playoff contention before they kicked off Sunday night. They then went out and made owner Jerry Jones proud, with a 26-24 victory over the playoff-chasing Tampa Bay Buccaneers.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: O'Connell passes for 257 yards and Raiders snap 10-game skid with 19-14 win over Jaguars
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Aidan O'Connell returned from an injury and passed for 257 yards, and the Las Vegas Raiders ended a 10-game losing streak with a 19-14 victory over the Jacksonville Jaguars on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bears vs. Lions: Injuries, inactives and key matchups for Sunday's game at Soldier Field
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Lions have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. See INJURY REPORT below. CHICAGO — The Bears are "playing out the string," as they say, eliminated from NFL playoff contention with three games still left to play this season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Oakland legend, Hall of Famer Rickey Henderson has died at the age of 65
item.description: (KRON) – Rickey Henderson, one of the greatest baseball players to ever live, died at age 65. Henderson is Major League Baseball’s all-time stolen base leader and is considered by many to be the greatest player in A’s franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: UCSD basketball heating up, Tritons on a tear after historic upset
item.description: Fresh after pulling off what might be one of the biggest wins in program history, UCSD men's basketball has no plans on slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Guenther scores twice as Utah HC beats Minnesota, 2-1
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is in seventh heaven right about now. Dylan Guenther scored twice, including the game-winner at 10:01 of the third period, as Utah ran its road winning streak to seven in a row Friday night with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over the Minnesota Wild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Pro pickleball players now earn more than WNBA, NWSL players
item.description: (KRON) -- The pickleball craze that's swept across the Bay Area and nation in recent years has apparently paid off, especially for female athletes. According to the Professional Pickleball Association, pro female players on the PPA Tour earned an average of $260,000 in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Bulls beat Celtics 117-108 in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Celtics forward Jaylen Brown took issue with back-to-back technical fouls called on coach Joe Mazzulla and himself late in the fourth quarter of Boston’s 117-108 loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: San Diego FC releases 2025 regular season schedule
item.description: San Diego FC will be kicking off its first regular season match in franchise history in February against a rival city.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs' OC Liam Coen addresses NFL head coaching rumors
item.description: Tampa Bay Buccaneers' first-year offensive coordinator Liam Coen has been excellent calling plays for the Bucs this season.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Boys Basketball: La Jolla 55, Army-Navy 47
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in La Jolla with the Vikings hosting Army-Navy from Carlsbad. Great game that went right down to the wire, La Jolla won 52-47. Vikings were lead by Leo Hawkinson with 16. Warriors were lead by Yanler Ramos game high 26 points.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa native named starting QB for Atlanta Falcons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gimmicks, not football, dominating college bowl season
item.description: College bowl games are becoming less about football and more about marketing in an ever-changing sports landscape
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Isaac Wilson decides to return to Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Upon second thought, Isaac Wilson is returning to Utah. Eight days after announcing he was entering the transfer portal, Wilson announced that he is returning to Utah, where will compete for the starting quarterback job with New Mexico transfer Devon Dampier.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs with Bowles on team's December rise and a dominating win against the Chargers
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers continued to add pressure in the NFC South title race with a commanding 40-17 win over the Los Angeles Chargers.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC
